news_summary: MEPs are accelerating efforts to support farmers by addressing the challenges of rising fertiliser prices.
news_summary_fr: Les députés européens redoublent d'efforts pour soutenir les agriculteurs en s'attaquant aux défis posés par la hausse des prix des engrais.
news_summary_es: Los eurodiputados están intensificando sus esfuerzos para apoyar a los agricultores haciendo frente a los retos que plantea el aumento de los precios de los fertilizantes.

articles.title: Press release - Fertiliser prices: MEPs fast-track work on farmer support
articles.summary: On Tuesday, Parliament agreed to use the urgent procedure for measures to support farmers affected by rising fertiliser prices.Committee on Agriculture and Rural Development Source : © European Union, 2026 - EP
articles.content:
The proposed measures were first announced by the Commission in its fertiliser action plan. They include a liquidity scheme and advanced direct payments for farmers to help them pay banks and suppliers. Member States would also be able to adjust their allocations for direct payments for 2027 to avoid farmers who have not yet purchased fertilisers changing their crop choices for the next sowing season.



Next steps

Parliament is expected to vote on the substance of the proposal during the July plenary session.



Background

The EU relies on imports for 30% of the nitrogen-based fertilisers and 70% of the phosphatic fertilisers used for agricultural production, while production in the EU relies on natural gas. Prices of both, together with energy prices, have been growing due to recent geopolitical events, such as the closure of the closure of the Strait of Hormuz and Russia’s war of aggression against Ukraine.
articles.reasoning: The article reports on the European Parliament fast-tracking support measures for farmers facing rising fertiliser prices, which is a significant policy action with broad impact on agricultural stability and food security in the EU. It focuses on concrete financial support mechanisms to help farmers manage increased costs, addressing a real-world economic challenge affecting many people. Although the article is brief, it provides sufficient context about the problem and the proposed solutions, making it a meaningful positive development.
articles.personality_title: EU Parliament speeds up support for farmers facing rising fertiliser costs
articles.personality_presentation:
**Context** – Fertiliser prices in the European Union have been rising because of higher energy costs and difficulties in importing key materials. This is a problem for farmers who need fertilisers to grow crops.

**What happened** – On Tuesday, the European Parliament decided to use a faster process to approve new support measures for farmers. These measures include financial help like loans and early payments to cover costs from banks and suppliers. Countries in the EU can also change how they give direct payments to farmers in 2027 to help those who have not yet bought fertilisers avoid changing their planting plans.

**Impact** – This fast action is important because it helps farmers manage rising costs without having to change what they grow. It supports farmers’ stability and helps protect food production across the EU during uncertain times.

**What's next step** – The Parliament will vote on these support measures in July. If approved, the help can reach farmers soon to ease their financial pressure.

**One-sentence takeaway** – The EU Parliament is quickly moving to provide financial support to farmers affected by rising fertiliser prices to protect farming and food supply.
articles.personality_title_fr: Le Parlement européen accélère le soutien aux agriculteurs face à la hausse des prix des engrais
articles.personality_presentation_fr:
**Contexte** – Les prix des engrais dans l’Union européenne augmentent à cause de la hausse des coûts de l’énergie et des difficultés d’importation des matières premières. Cela pose un problème pour les agriculteurs qui ont besoin d’engrais pour leurs cultures.

**Ce qui s'est passé** – Mardi, le Parlement européen a choisi une procédure accélérée pour approuver de nouvelles mesures de soutien aux agriculteurs. Ces mesures comprennent une aide financière comme des prêts et des paiements anticipés pour couvrir les coûts auprès des banques et des fournisseurs. Les pays de l’UE peuvent aussi modifier leurs paiements directs en 2027 pour aider les agriculteurs qui n’ont pas encore acheté d’engrais à ne pas changer leurs plans de culture.

**Impact** – Cette action rapide est importante car elle aide les agriculteurs à gérer la hausse des coûts sans changer leurs cultures. Elle soutient la stabilité des agriculteurs et protège la production alimentaire dans l’UE en période d’incertitude.

**Prochaine étape** – Le Parlement votera ces mesures de soutien en juillet. Si elles sont approuvées, l’aide pourra rapidement atteindre les agriculteurs pour alléger leur pression financière.

**Résumé en une phrase** – Le Parlement européen agit rapidement pour soutenir financièrement les agriculteurs touchés par la hausse des prix des engrais afin de protéger l’agriculture et l’approvisionnement alimentaire.
articles.personality_title_es: El Parlamento Europeo acelera el apoyo a agricultores ante el aumento de precios de fertilizantes
articles.personality_presentation_es:
**Contexto** – Los precios de los fertilizantes en la Unión Europea han subido debido al aumento de los costos de energía y problemas para importar materiales clave. Esto afecta a los agricultores que necesitan fertilizantes para sus cultivos.

**Qué pasó** – El martes, el Parlamento Europeo decidió usar un proceso rápido para aprobar nuevas medidas de apoyo a los agricultores. Estas incluyen ayuda financiera como préstamos y pagos anticipados para cubrir costos con bancos y proveedores. Los países de la UE también pueden ajustar los pagos directos en 2027 para ayudar a agricultores que aún no han comprado fertilizantes a no cambiar sus planes de cultivo.

**Impacto** – Esta acción rápida es importante porque ayuda a los agricultores a manejar los costos crecientes sin cambiar sus cultivos. Apoya la estabilidad de los agricultores y protege la producción de alimentos en la UE durante tiempos difíciles.

**Próximo paso** – El Parlamento votará estas medidas en julio. Si se aprueban, la ayuda llegará pronto a los agricultores para aliviar su presión financiera.

**Resumen en una frase** – El Parlamento Europeo está acelerando el apoyo financiero a agricultores afectados por el aumento en los precios de fertilizantes para proteger la agricultura y el suministro de alimentos.
articles.image_prompt: A detailed, warm painting of a flourishing farm field with golden crops growing under a soft sky, featuring a symbolic hand gently holding a glowing sack of fertiliser beside a stylized bank building made of natural stone, connected by a flowing stream of coins transforming into nutrient-rich soil, all rendered in natural, simple earth tones and soft greens.
